title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between James William Kalnas
meeting_title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Leonid Shvorob and James William Kalnas

transcript.text: Hi, Jim?
Can you hear me?
Hi, how are you?
Oh, just try and stay out of troubles.
How are you?
Very good, thank you.
Yeah, so yeah, awesome.
Let's dive in.

So I was checking your website and like you're already more than 30 years in recruiting is really great, you know, so usually, yeah, it's like 10 or five or more, but this, yeah, so I want to ask you, what's roles in engineering or manufacturing or what's your main focus now is more easy easy to feel and give you the most profit.
To rephrase what your question was, you want me to outline whether I'm doing manufacturing or engineering now and what my focus is today?
Is that what you're asking?
Yeah, exactly.
Okay, yeah.
So, yeah, we do a lot of engineering and manufacturing and operations.
We have several vertical markets.
They'd be steel, aluminum, aerospace, construction, chemicals, mining, all of those.
We have a lot of experience.
And I do a lot of the retained search for our company.
I just placed the general manager at Universal Stainless Steel up in Dunkirk, New York.
I placed their melt shop manager down in Ridgeville.
I just placed a non-destructive test level three technician with ultrasonic experience at Arconic.
I have a metallurgist interviewing on Friday.
They wanted someone with aluminum experience.
I've done controller positions for a mining company in sales as well as operations.
I've placed the president at Davis Wire recently as well as the president at National Standard.
My company, we do primarily engineering and operations.
My background is automation.
Prior to starting this company, I was with a company that was called ESA, Eclectic System Architectures.
I was the third employee.
We built them up from three employees to 130 employees between Pittsburgh and Cleveland, doing level one PLC, SCADA, and supervisory control work, some embedded control systems work.

I started this company in 98, and all my automation friends got promoted, and they talked me into going into mechanical and project management and schedulers and metallurgical engineers, mechanical engineers.
a good ち Fighting leur Hey, love you, Mike.
I placed the Vice President of Reliability Engineering with JSW Steel in Baytown, Texas, as well as Mingo Junction last year.
So that gives you a little thumbnail of what I do.
Presently, I'm working on a sales position for a specialty bar company.
They do coal-drawn bar, and I just got a retainer on that.
So we've placed people in 27 states so far in five countries.
We've done a lot of work in graphite, well, for the companies that make the 9-foot-long graphite electrodes.
I've placed three quality engineers with them in Mexico, Germany, and Cleveland.
So it just gives you a little idea of what I do.
Yeah, it sounds huge.
Thank you.
I'm going to lie.
I appreciate it.
So, you're saying, so mostly the most roles, you're focusing...
Right now, it's more easy to feel and like just more good.
It's like sales in bar companies or is it retained executive search or?
You want to know if I've done sales?
Is that correct?
No, no, no.
Like, so you say you're working in a lot of companies, like five countries, you're working with a lot of roles.
But if you can choose, what would be your main focus for this quarter?
For this quarter?
Yeah.
I'm about to get a general manager position down in West Virginia for a greenfield facility.
Mm-hmm.
So that, I mean, it's kind of neat because I get to do different things and I'm always learning something.
So, and I have great advisors who have really good experience.
I have one of my advisors has a PhD in, I'm sorry, metallurgical engineering from Carnegie Mellon.
I have, he used to be their COO.
His name's Kevin Handerhan.
I have another fellow that used to be with our.
RTI, which is a titanium company, and I placed director of engineering with him, and he became an advisor.
He's the one that introduced me to Arconic and helped me out with that.
I got Peter Barnard, who used to be the president of GrafTech.
They make the electrodes, and he retired in 2006.
He was their president.
And these guys, they don't want to really retire.
They get bored.
So they like to work part-time with me.
So that's the kind of stuff I do.
I just finished a couple of retainers with Arconic.
I'm working with the Keystone Profiles, which is the cold-drawn bar company for their sales director.
And I'm expecting to get the GM position here shortly.
That's great.
Yeah.
So, it sounds...

Sounds like you have a very huge network, and people you help all over the way, they always recommend you, so you have like established name in industry, but mostly of your clients get from your network, from your referrals, or you have some systematic way to find new clients?
Well, I'm the sales guy in the company, and I get a lot of referrals.
mean, I've been doing this 36 years now, and I'm constantly networking, and that's how I find a lot of our contracts.
I've worked with stainless steel companies, aluminum companies, engineering companies like Prime Metals, TanovaCore, the people that sell the furnaces.
I do a lot of work in the chemical industry.
I'm just, I'm the sales guy, and I have six contingent recruiters that work with me.
Mm-hmm.
Okay, so mostly
Basically, it's for recommendations from networking, right?
Right, pretty much.
Okay, yeah, that's good.
So is it always stable or it can be like one quarter you have a lot of clients and other quarter like phone not rings and it's not so stable?
I do my best to keep it steady.
Sometimes it gets a little lumpy where you have a whole bunch of sales and then you have a slow month because you have to rebuild all of the work orders as you close them.
But we've been pretty good at keeping a steady workflow in the company.
Okay, that's great.
So may I ask, what makes you like to jump on this call?
If like everything is doing all great, you're just curious or you have some goals for this quarter so you think you will need extra help?
Or is that what this call is about that you guys, that you're looking to recruit under my umbrella?
Is that what this call is about or are you looking for someone to help you close?
What or those positions that you already have?
Okay, so I'm not a recruitment company, so we're working with recruiters and we help them connecting with companies who are actively hiring.
Okay.
Yeah, I'd be interested to see more about your company.
I'm always looking to grow my company and I'm always looking to move into new organizations.
You know, I'm the little guy, right?
So I typically go by recommendations.
mean, that's how I can get into new accounts is through a recommendation, which gives me credibility as opposed to a big national company, like an Aerotech or something like that.
So it's usually by references.
I do some work with Hydric and Struggles, too.
I don't know if you're familiar with them.
They're a fully retained recruiting firm.
was your screen like
I in Chicago, so they've given me a few introductions as well.
Mm-hmm.
Does that make sense?
Yeah, so you say you want to work with bigger companies with more interesting roles, so kind of like this, right?
Yes.
And as I see, it's not like you cannot say your referrals, hey, can you me recommend next month to these guys?
So it doesn't work like that, right?
Thank you.
I'm sorry.
Yeah, mean, for example, I was referred to a company called Marifab by one of my contacts.
They want me to find them a new COO.
You know, I get recommendations all the time.
Mm-hmm.
Okay, that's great.
Yeah.
So do you have, like, specific goals, like, for this quarter?
Or it's just like curious how you can get more leads or I'm looking to generate about 80,000, 80,000 in revenues per month as my goal.
Yeah, that's good.
So now is how much?
If it's not a super, I'm sorry.
So your goal is 80K.
So for now is how much?
So I want to see.
Yeah, made 80K this month in January.
We made 80K in December.
January is looking pretty good right now.
You know, in my business, you can't see out more than two months.
Right?
I mean, it's just the way it is.
You're just constantly churning and getting new retainers and closing some contingent business along the way.
Yeah, it makes sense.
So they won't just keep the pipeline going or you want to grow or you
It's hard because we're a virtual company.
All of my recruiters are independent, except for one.
They all have their own little companies.
And I do all the sales and I bring them in as I need to help with certain contingent openings.
And then I do all the retained work with my son, Dan.
Okay, I see.
I mean, I could send you a PowerPoint on the company if that makes any sense.
But, you know, it's a nice introduction on what we do.
Okay, okay, I see.
So, Jim, you know, I now don't see like this is like there is some urgency because you say you're doing pretty well and you're all good with your referrals or I keep it wrong?
I'm always looking to build my network for sure.
Because there's always people change companies.
And then the new HR person comes in and they want to bring their people in.
So, you're always looking for new introductions.
you.
Yeah, because usually we help in recruiting companies who need to grow or want a more sustainable channel, you know, because...
Where are you located?
I'm now in Bali.
In Bali?
Yeah, it's nights like 1 a.m., but it's a good time.
We're talking with good people.
I think it's 27 degrees here.
No, it's less than that.
It's very cold here.
It was 5 degrees Fahrenheit last night.
Today we're going to warm up to...
Oh, it's 30 degrees now.
Yeah.
it's get cold again, and we have snow on the ground here, so it's very different from where you are.
Yeah, I see your clothes say...
Yeah, it's cold out.
Yeah, it's actually cold out.
Yeah, so we have a huge rain season now, so it's always raining, especially at night.
Okay.
Yeah.
Well, it was nice talking to you.
I can send you more information on my company, and then if you'd send stuff to me, I'd appreciate
Yeah, so just making this clear, as I see, you don't need more clients, more predictable pipeline in the next few months.
Is it something that would interest in and you want I tell you more or it's not a priority right now?
I'd be interested in learning more about your company if you want to send me some stuff.
That'd be great.
Once I receive it, I'll send you more in my company and we can decide if there's some kind of a match.
So I think we're already in the call and I can tell you how we're working with our processes and you can say if it looks valuable for you or we can park it here.
How it sounds?
Yeah, I just got a phone call from a customer, though, that came in.
He just got off a flight in Charlotte and he wanted to talk to me.
So can you send me some stuff and I'll send it back to you, my stuff, and then we can reschedule?
Okay, think just sending stuff with back and forth is not so good, but we can reschedule.
So you want to, yeah, I will send you other time.
Would you be?
open this week, or what's your schedule?
This week would be fine, and once I receive the invitation, I'll send you my PowerPoint.
Okay, okay, that's great.
Great, thank you.
Yeah, see you.
Bye-bye.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To assess if Partners Alliance needs SystemHustle's client-generation services.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strong Performance:** Partners Alliance is meeting its $80k/month revenue goal, driven by Jim Kalnas's 36-year network and retained executive searches.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=596.0)
  - [**Referral-Based Model:** The business is entirely referral-based, which provides high-credibility access to clients and enables a lean, virtual structure.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [**No Immediate Need:** The business model is stable and successful, leaving no urgent need for SystemHustle's services to generate new clients.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=694.0)
  - [**Rescheduled for Info Exchange:** The call ended early due to a client call. It was rescheduled for a mutual information exchange to explore a potential future fit.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=805.0)

## Topics

### Partners Alliance: Business Model & Performance

  - [**Primary Focus:** Retained executive search in engineering, manufacturing, and operations.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=74.0)
  - [**Verticals:** Steel, aluminum, aerospace, construction, chemicals, and mining.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=74.0)
  - [**Recent Placements:**](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=92.0)
      - [General Manager (Universal Stainless Steel)](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=92.0)
      - [Melt Shop Manager (Universal Stainless Steel)](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
      - [President (Davis Wire, National Standard)](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
      - [VP of Reliability Engineering (JSW Steel)](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
      - [Non-Destructive Test (NDT) Level 3 Technician (Arconic)](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [**Current Pipeline:**](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=193.0)
      - [Sales Director (Keystone Profiles)](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
      - [GM for a greenfield facility (West Virginia)](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=260.0)
  - [**Revenue:** Consistently hitting the $80k/month goal (Jan & Dec).](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=596.0)
  - [**Structure:**](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=662.0)
      - [Jim Kalnas → Sales & Retained Search (with son, Dan)](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=662.0)
      - [6 independent contingent recruiters](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=411.0)

### Client Acquisition: Referral-Based Strategy

  - [**Primary Source:** 36 years of networking and referrals.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [**Rationale:** Referrals provide instant credibility, which is essential for a small firm competing with national agencies.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)
  - [**Key Advisors:** A network of industry veterans (e.g., former COO of GrafTech, PhD metallurgist) who provide introductions.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=280.0)
  - [**Partnerships:** Receives introductions from larger firms like Heidrick & Struggles.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=532.0)

### SystemHustle's Offer & Outcome

  - [**SystemHustle's Offer:** A service to connect recruiters with companies that are actively hiring, aiming to provide a more sustainable client pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=484.0)
  - [**Outcome:** The call ended early due to a client call for Jim Kalnas.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=815.0)
  - [**Decision:** Reschedule for a mutual information exchange to explore a potential fit, as no immediate need was identified.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=805.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Leonid Shvorob:** Send a calendar invite for a follow-up call this week.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=829.0)
  - [**Jim Kalnas:** Send the Partners Alliance company PowerPoint after receiving the invite.](https://fathom.video/share/kNyWvHs9QrspBiTfh13xyLH4dcBR3Vwt?tab=summary&timestamp=829.0)

action_items.description: Email Jim company deck/materials; request his deck in reply
Send Jim calendar invite for follow-up call this week
